cases.summary: FileHelper, DocxHelper, ExcelHelper, HtmlHelper, XMLHelper, PDF helpers, and other parser/converter internals are not standalone shipped capabilities unless a public step annotation exposes them directly.
cases.reason: The reviewed module contains substantial helper-driven behavior for file lookup, archive extraction, document parsing, PDF extraction, JSON mutation, and XML traversal, but the runtime bundle contract is curated from annotated public step methods only.

cases.summary: README descriptions, format lists, and process descriptions do not expand the shipped capability contract.
cases.reason: The reviewed README lists module breadth and examples, but runtime truth remains limited to public annotated methods in the reviewed step classes.

cases.summary: This bundle does not promise universal support for arbitrary file, document, spreadsheet, archive, image, html, json, pdf, or xml formats.
cases.reason: Reviewed support is limited to the exact annotated step families and their narrow source-backed semantics. The module is not promoted here as a generic document-processing platform.

cases.summary: The bundle does not promise arbitrary filesystem orchestration, directory management, copy/move/delete pipelines, or generic attachment workflows beyond the reviewed steps.
cases.reason: The reviewed file steps cover a narrow set of local file-object, file-content, download-check, zip-unpack, and list-selection behaviors only.

cases.summary: Download-related steps must not be treated as generic browser automation or robust download orchestration.
cases.reason: The reviewed implementation checks one configured download directory, waits up to 5 seconds, matches by filename substring, and then either copies the found file or deletes the original downloaded file.

cases.summary: DOCX support is limited to the reviewed template substitution, text-content equality, and text-content extraction steps.
cases.reason: The reviewed DOCX steps do not expose generic Word editing, styling assertions, section/header/footer semantics, comments, tracked changes, or arbitrary document generation flows.

cases.summary: Excel support is limited to the reviewed lookup, assertion, JSON-conversion, and one fill-color-check family.
cases.reason: The reviewed Excel steps do not expose arbitrary workbook editing, formula recalculation control, chart handling, workbook generation, or broad style inspection beyond the explicit fill-color step.

cases.summary: HTML support is limited to parsing an HTML string, selecting one element by XPath, and reading own text or one attribute value from the stored element.
cases.reason: The reviewed HTML steps do not expose browser execution, CSS selection, DOM mutation, JavaScript evaluation, page loading, or generic scraping semantics beyond those three annotated methods.

cases.summary: Image support is limited to loading an image from a path and comparing two stored images with the reviewed diff semantics.
cases.reason: The reviewed image steps do not expose cropping, masking, threshold tuning, OCR, metadata inspection, or generic image-processing pipelines.

cases.summary: JSON support must not be generalized into a full document database, schema engine, or HTTP platform abstraction.
cases.reason: The reviewed JSON steps cover specific JsonPath/GPath extraction, equality, transformation, array capture, comparison, and response-body lookup families only. They do not promise generic schema validation, arbitrary query engines, or cross-module response semantics beyond the annotated methods here.

cases.summary: PDF support is limited to the reviewed text, text-slice, PDF-to-image, and table-assertion families.
cases.reason: The reviewed PDF steps do not expose generic layout extraction, page rendering controls, annotations, signatures, form-field semantics, arbitrary region OCR, or universal PDF understanding.

cases.summary: XML support is limited to the reviewed tag, attribute, node-list, xmlpath-array, and escape steps.
cases.reason: The reviewed XML steps do not expose XSD validation, namespace-aware schema workflows, XPath mutation, XSLT transforms, or generic XML authoring semantics.
